untranslatable_reason: This signal requires factor-based stock ranking (WML: Winner-Minus-Loser momentum factor) and cross-sectional portfolio construction across a large-cap Indian stock universe with monthly rebalancing. The allowed indicators are price-based technical indicators only—there is no support for: (1) factor scoring/ranking, (2) percentile/quintile/decile selection across a universe, (3) market-cap filtering, or (4) multi-leg portfolio weighting. A true implementation requires portfolio construction logic beyond single-security condition evaluation.
translation_notes: This is a factor-based, cross-sectional stock selection strategy (long-only momentum portfolio with WML tilt), not a time-series technical signal on individual securities. It cannot be expressed using the allowed technical indicators. A backtest engine supporting this signal would need: (i) factor library with WML calculation, (ii) universe filtering by market cap, (iii) ranking/sorting across N stocks, (iv) quintile/decile selection, and (v) portfolio-level rebalancing logic. The 7.34%-7.75% annualized alpha suggests strong historical outperformance on Indian large-caps (2013-2019), but implementation requires a factor/cross-sectional backtester, not a technical rule engine.
